package com.werken.saxpath;

import org.junit.Test;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;
import com.werken.saxpath.Token;
import com.werken.saxpath.XPathLexer;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunner;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunnerParameters;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(EvoRunner.class)
@EvoRunnerParameters(mockJVMNonDeterminism = true, useVFS = true, useVNET = true, resetStaticState = true, separateClassLoader = false)
public class XPathLexer_ESTest extends XPathLexer_ESTest_scaffolding {

    // Constants for magic numbers
    private static final int INITIAL_POSITION = 0;
    private static final int UNICODE_EOF = '\uFFFF';

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testLiteralAdvancesPosition() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with a specific string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("*>=@tq*=5");

        // When: Calling the literal method
        lexer.literal();

        // Then: The current position should be 7
        assertEquals("Current position should be 7 after literal", 7, lexer.currentPosition());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testIsIdentifierStartCharWithBackslash() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with a specific string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("com.werken.saxpath.XPathLexer");

        // When: Checking if backslash is an identifier start character
        boolean isIdentifierStart = lexer.isIdentifierStartChar('\\');

        // Then: The current position should be 0 and backslash should not be an identifier start character
        assertEquals("Current position should be 0 initially", INITIAL_POSITION, lexer.currentPosition());
        assertFalse("Backslash should not be an identifier start character", isIdentifierStart);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testIsIdentifierCharWithSpace() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with a specific string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("com.werken.saxpath.XPathLexer");

        // When: Checking if space is an identifier character
        lexer.isIdentifierChar(' ');

        // Then: The current position should remain 0
        assertEquals("Current position should remain 0", INITIAL_POSITION, lexer.currentPosition());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testHasMoreChars() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with a specific string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("|;@4dvCO.yQ,c");

        // When: Checking if there are more characters
        boolean hasMore = lexer.hasMoreChars();

        // Then: The current position should be 0, end position should be 13, and there should be more characters
        assertEquals("Current position should be 0 initially", INITIAL_POSITION, lexer.currentPosition());
        assertEquals("End position should be 13", 13, lexer.endPosition());
        assertTrue("There should be more characters", hasMore);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetXPathReturnsNull() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An uninitialized XPathLexer
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer();

        // When: Getting the XPath
        String xPath = lexer.getXPath();

        // Then: The XPath should be null
        assertNull("XPath should be null", xPath);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetPreviousTokenReturnsNull() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An uninitialized XPathLexer
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer();

        // When: Getting the previous token
        Token previousToken = lexer.getPreviousToken();

        // Then: The previous token should be null
        assertNull("Previous token should be null", previousToken);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testEndPositionInitiallyZero() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An uninitialized XPathLexer
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer();

        // When: Getting the end position
        int endPosition = lexer.endPosition();

        // Then: The end position should be 0
        assertEquals("End position should be 0 initially", INITIAL_POSITION, endPosition);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCurrentPositionInitiallyZero() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with an empty string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("");

        // When: Getting the current position
        int currentPosition = lexer.currentPosition();

        // Then: The current position should be 0
        assertEquals("Current position should be 0 initially", INITIAL_POSITION, currentPosition);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCommaAdvancesPosition() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with a specific string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("*>=@tq*=5");

        // When: Calling the comma method
        lexer.comma();

        // Then: The current position should be 1
        assertEquals("Current position should be 1 after comma", 1, lexer.currentPosition());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testLookAheadBeyondEndReturnsEOF() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An XPathLexer initialized with a specific string
        XPathLexer lexer = new XPathLexer("t%");

        // When: Looking ahead 8 characters
        char lookAheadChar = lexer.LA(8);

        // Then: The end position should be 2, current position should be 0, and look ahead should return EOF
        assertEquals("End position should be 2", 2, lexer.endPosition());
        assertEquals("Current position should be 0 initially", INITIAL_POSITION, lexer.currentPosition());
        assertEquals("Look ahead should return EOF", UNICODE_EOF, lookAheadChar);
    }

    // Additional refactored tests would follow the same pattern...

}

import org.junit.Test;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;

import static org.evosuite.runtime.EvoAssertions.*;
import java.util.Collection;
import java.util.Comparator;
import java.util.LinkedList;
import org.apache.commons.lang3.builder.CompareToBuilder;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunner;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunnerParameters;
import org.evosuite.runtime.ViolatedAssumptionAnswer;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(EvoRunner.class)
@EvoRunnerParameters(mockJVMNonDeterminism = true, useVFS = true, useVNET = true, resetStaticState = true, separateClassLoader = false)
public class CompareToBuilder_ESTest extends CompareToBuilder_ESTest_scaffolding {

    private static final int EXPECTED_EQUAL = 0;
    private static final int EXPECTED_LESS_THAN = -1;
    private static final int EXPECTED_GREATER_THAN = 1;

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testInitialComparisonIsZero() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CompareToBuilder instance
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();

        // When: No comparisons are appended
        int result = compareToBuilder.toComparison();

        // Then: The comparison result should be zero
        assertEquals("Initial comparison should be zero", EXPECTED_EQUAL, result);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testBooleanArrayComparison() throws Throwable {
        // Given: Two boolean arrays of different lengths
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();
        boolean[] shorterArray = new boolean[1];
        boolean[] longerArray = new boolean[7];

        // When: Comparing the arrays
        compareToBuilder.append(longerArray, shorterArray);

        // Then: The comparison result should indicate the first array is greater
        assertEquals("Longer array should be greater", EXPECTED_GREATER_THAN, (int) compareToBuilder.build());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testBooleanArrayComparisonWithNull() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A null boolean array and a non-null boolean array
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();
        boolean[] nonNullArray = new boolean[0];

        // When: Comparing a null array with a non-null array
        compareToBuilder.append((boolean[]) null, nonNullArray);

        // Then: The comparison result should indicate the first array is less
        assertEquals("Null array should be less", EXPECTED_LESS_THAN, (int) compareToBuilder.build());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testFloatArrayComparison() throws Throwable {
        // Given: Two float arrays with different first elements
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();
        float[] arrayWithNegativeValue = new float[9];
        arrayWithNegativeValue[0] = -984.22424F;
        float[] zeroArray = new float[9];

        // When: Comparing the arrays
        compareToBuilder.append(arrayWithNegativeValue, zeroArray);

        // Then: The comparison result should indicate the first array is less
        assertEquals("Array with negative value should be less", EXPECTED_LESS_THAN, (int) compareToBuilder.build());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testDoubleArrayComparisonWithNull() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A non-null double array and a null double array
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();
        double[] nonNullArray = new double[2];

        // When: Comparing a non-null array with a null array
        compareToBuilder.append(nonNullArray, (double[]) null);

        // Then: The comparison result should indicate the first array is greater
        assertEquals("Non-null array should be greater", EXPECTED_GREATER_THAN, compareToBuilder.toComparison());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testByteArrayComparison() throws Throwable {
        // Given: Two byte arrays with different first elements
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();
        byte[] arrayWithNegativeValue = new byte[6];
        arrayWithNegativeValue[1] = (byte) -100;
        byte[] zeroArray = new byte[6];

        // When: Comparing the arrays
        compareToBuilder.append(arrayWithNegativeValue, zeroArray);

        // Then: The comparison result should indicate the first array is less
        assertEquals("Array with negative value should be less", EXPECTED_LESS_THAN, compareToBuilder.toComparison());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testReflectionCompareDifferentStrings() throws Throwable {
        // Given: Two different strings
        String string1 = "'H.gXx*G(,";
        String string2 = "HO$umf_h";

        // When: Using reflectionCompare to compare the strings
        int result = CompareToBuilder.reflectionCompare(string1, string2, (String[]) null);

        // Then: The comparison result should indicate the first string is greater
        assertEquals("First string should be greater", EXPECTED_GREATER_THAN, result);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testReflectionCompareSameObject() throws Throwable {
        // Given: The same object for both parameters
        Object object = new Object();

        // When: Using reflectionCompare to compare the same object
        int result = CompareToBuilder.reflectionCompare(object, object);

        // Then: The comparison result should be zero
        assertEquals("Comparison of the same object should be zero", EXPECTED_EQUAL, result);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testAppendSuper() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A CompareToBuilder with a super comparison value
        CompareToBuilder compareToBuilder = new CompareToBuilder();

        // When: Appending a super comparison value
        compareToBuilder.appendSuper(6);

        // Then: The comparison result should reflect the super value
        assertEquals("Super comparison value should be reflected", 6, compareToBuilder.toComparison());
    }

    // Additional refactored tests would follow the same pattern...

}

package org.joda.time.field;

import org.junit.Test;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;
import static org.evosuite.runtime.EvoAssertions.*;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunner;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunnerParameters;
import org.joda.time.DateTimeFieldType;
import org.joda.time.DateTimeZone;
import org.joda.time.DurationField;
import org.joda.time.DurationFieldType;
import org.joda.time.Minutes;
import org.joda.time.Seconds;
import org.joda.time.chrono.JulianChronology;
import org.joda.time.chrono.ZonedChronology;
import org.joda.time.field.UnsupportedDurationField;
import org.joda.time.tz.FixedDateTimeZone;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(EvoRunner.class) @EvoRunnerParameters(mockJVMNonDeterminism = true, useVFS = true, useVNET = true, resetStaticState = true, separateClassLoader = false)
public class UnsupportedDurationField_ESTest extends UnsupportedDurationField_ESTest_scaffolding {

  @Test(timeout = 4000)
  public void testUnsupportedDurationFieldIsNotSupported() throws Throwable {
    // Given: An instance of UnsupportedDurationField with null DurationFieldType
    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance((DurationFieldType) null);

    // When: Checking if the field is supported
    boolean isSupported = unsupportedDurationField.isSupported();

    // Then: The field should not be supported
    assertFalse("The field should not be supported", isSupported);
  }

  @Test(timeout = 4000)

    // Given: An instance of UnsupportedDurationField with DurationFieldType.hours
    DurationFieldType durationFieldType = DurationFieldType.hours();
    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance(durationFieldType);

  @Test(timeout = 4000)
  public void testGetNameReturnsSeconds() throws Throwable {
    // Given: An instance of UnsupportedDurationField with DurationFieldType.seconds
    Seconds seconds = Seconds.MAX_VALUE;

    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance(durationFieldType);

    // When: Getting the name of the field
    String name = unsupportedDurationField.getName();

    // Then: The name should be "seconds"

  }

  @Test(timeout = 4000)
  public void testGetInstanceOfZonedChronology() throws Throwable {
    // Given: JulianChronology instance and UTC DateTimeZone
    JulianChronology julianChronology = JulianChronology.getInstanceUTC();
    FixedDateTimeZone fixedDateTimeZone = (FixedDateTimeZone) DateTimeZone.UTC;

    // When: Getting the instance of ZonedChronology
    ZonedChronology zonedChronology = ZonedChronology.getInstance(julianChronology, fixedDateTimeZone);

    // Then: The zonedChronology should not be null
    assertNotNull("The zonedChronology should not be null", zonedChronology);
  }

  @Test(timeout = 4000)
  public void testEqualsReturnsFalseForDifferentTypes() throws Throwable {
    // Given: An instance of UnsupportedDurationField with DurationFieldType.weekyears
    DurationFieldType durationFieldType = DurationFieldType.weekyears();

    // When: Comparing the field with a different type
    boolean isEqual = unsupportedDurationField.equals(durationFieldType);

    // Then: The fields should not be equal

  }

  @Test(timeout = 4000)

    // Given: An instance of UnsupportedDurationField with null DurationFieldType
    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance((DurationFieldType) null);

    // Then: The fields should be equal

  }

  @Test(timeout = 4000)
  public void testEqualsReturnsFalseForDifferentInstances() throws Throwable {
    // Given: Two instances of UnsupportedDurationField with different DurationFieldTypes

    DurationFieldType durationFieldType2 = DurationFieldType.weekyears();
    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField1 = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance(durationFieldType1);
    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField2 = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance(durationFieldType2);

    // When: Comparing the two fields
    boolean isEqual = unsupportedDurationField1.equals(unsupportedDurationField2);

    // Then: The fields should not be equal
    assertFalse("The fields should not be equal", isEqual);
  }

  @Test(timeout = 4000)
  public void testToStringReturnsUnsupportedDurationField() throws Throwable {
    // Given: An instance of UnsupportedDurationField with DurationFieldType.years

    UnsupportedDurationField unsupportedDurationField = UnsupportedDurationField.getInstance(durationFieldType);

    // When: Getting the string representation of the field
    String stringRepresentation = unsupportedDurationField.toString();

    // Then: The string representation should be "UnsupportedDurationField[years]"

  }

package org.apache.commons.cli;

import org.junit.Test;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;
import java.util.Iterator;
import java.util.List;
import org.apache.commons.cli.CommandLine;
import org.apache.commons.cli.Option;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunner;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunnerParameters;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(EvoRunner.class)
@EvoRunnerParameters(mockJVMNonDeterminism = true, useVFS = true, useVNET = true, resetStaticState = true, separateClassLoader = false)
public class CommandLine_ESTest extends CommandLine_ESTest_scaffolding {

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionValuesWithEmptyOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving option values for an empty option
        String[] optionValues = commandLine.getOptionValues("");

        // Then: The result should be null
        assertNull("Option values should be null for an empty option", optionValues);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testAddOptionWithoutOptionalArg() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance and an Option without optional argument
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();
        Option option = new Option((String) null, false, "*");

        // When: Adding the option to the command line
        commandLine.addOption(option);

        // Then: The option should not have an optional argument
        assertFalse("Option should not have an optional argument", option.hasOptionalArg());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionValueWithValidOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance and an Option with a value
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();
        Option option = new Option("", "\"Jd", true, "D1,L");
        option.addValue("org.apache.commons.cli.CommandLine");

        // When: Adding the option to the command line and retrieving its value
        commandLine.addOption(option);
        String optionValue = commandLine.getOptionValue("--", (String) null);

        // Then: The option value should be the added value
        assertNotNull("Option value should not be null", optionValue);
        assertEquals("Option value should be the added value", "org.apache.commons.cli.CommandLine", optionValue);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionValueWithInvalidOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance and an Option with a specific name
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();
        Option option = new Option(" ", " ", true, "*,Od<*udS<o(OWc");

        // When: Adding the option to the command line and retrieving its value
        commandLine.addOption(option);
        String optionValue = commandLine.getOptionValue(" ");

        // Then: The option value should be null
        assertNull("Option value should be null for an invalid option", optionValue);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionObjectWithEmptyOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance and an Option with a value
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();
        Option option = new Option("", "\"Jd", true, "D1,L");
        option.addValue("org.apache.commons.cli.CommandLine");

        // When: Adding the option to the command line and retrieving its object
        commandLine.addOption(option);
        Object optionObject = commandLine.getOptionObject("");

        // Then: The option object should be null
        assertNull("Option object should be null for an empty option", optionObject);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionObjectWithNoValueOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance and an Option without a value
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();
        Option option = new Option("", false, "");

        // When: Adding the option to the command line and retrieving its object
        commandLine.addOption(option);
        Object optionObject = commandLine.getOptionObject("");

        // Then: The option object should be null
        assertNull("Option object should be null for an option without a value", optionObject);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testHasOptionWithEmptyOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance and an Option without a value
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();
        Option option = new Option("", false, "");

        // When: Adding the option to the command line and checking if it has the option
        commandLine.addOption(option);
        boolean hasOption = commandLine.hasOption("");

        // Then: The command line should have the option
        assertTrue("Command line should have the option", hasOption);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testAddArg() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Adding an argument to the command line
        commandLine.addArg("NUK,JY");

        // Then: No assertions needed as the method does not return a value
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetArgsWithNoArguments() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving arguments from the command line
        String[] args = commandLine.getArgs();

        // Then: The arguments array should be empty
        assertEquals("Arguments array should be empty", 0, args.length);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionObjectWithNonExistentOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the option object for a non-existent option
        Object optionObject = commandLine.getOptionObject('z');

        // Then: The option object should be null
        assertNull("Option object should be null for a non-existent option", optionObject);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetArgListWithNoArguments() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the argument list from the command line
        List argList = commandLine.getArgList();

        // Then: The argument list should be empty
        assertTrue("Argument list should be empty", argList.isEmpty());
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionValueWithDefaultValue() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the option value with a default value
        String optionValue = commandLine.getOptionValue('p', "26|#HQ,Xt");

        // Then: The option value should be the default value
        assertNotNull("Option value should not be null", optionValue);
        assertEquals("Option value should be the default value", "26|#HQ,Xt", optionValue);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionValueWithNonExistentOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the option value for a non-existent option
        String optionValue = commandLine.getOptionValue('=');

        // Then: The option value should be null
        assertNull("Option value should be null for a non-existent option", optionValue);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionValuesWithNonExistentOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the option values for a non-existent option
        String[] optionValues = commandLine.getOptionValues('d');

        // Then: The option values should be null
        assertNull("Option values should be null for a non-existent option", optionValues);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testIteratorWithNoArguments() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the iterator from the command line
        Iterator iterator = commandLine.iterator();

        // Then: The iterator should not be null
        assertNotNull("Iterator should not be null", iterator);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetOptionsWithNoOptions() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Retrieving the options from the command line
        Option[] options = commandLine.getOptions();

        // Then: The options array should be empty
        assertEquals("Options array should be empty", 0, options.length);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testHasOptionWithNonExistentOption() throws Throwable {
        // Given: A new CommandLine instance
        CommandLine commandLine = new CommandLine();

        // When: Checking if the command line has a non-existent option
        boolean hasOption = commandLine.hasOption('E');

        // Then: The command line should not have the option
        assertFalse("Command line should not have the option", hasOption);
    }
}

package org.apache.commons.compress.archivers;

import org.junit.Test;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;
import static org.evosuite.runtime.EvoAssertions.*;
import java.io.BufferedInputStream;
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream;
import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream;
import java.io.DataInputStream;
import java.io.FileDescriptor;
import java.io.InputStream;
import java.io.OutputStream;
import java.io.PipedInputStream;
import java.io.PipedOutputStream;
import org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveInputStream;
import org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory;
import org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ar.ArArchiveOutputStream;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunner;
import org.evosuite.runtime.EvoRunnerParameters;
import org.evosuite.runtime.mock.java.io.MockFileInputStream;
import org.evosuite.runtime.mock.java.io.MockFileOutputStream;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(EvoRunner.class)
@EvoRunnerParameters(mockJVMNonDeterminism = true, useVFS = true, useVNET = true, resetStaticState = true, separateClassLoader = false)
public class ArchiveStreamFactory_ESTest extends ArchiveStreamFactory_ESTest_scaffolding {

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveInputStreamWithPipedInputStream() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a PipedInputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        PipedInputStream pipedInputStream = new PipedInputStream();

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveInputStream with the PipedInputStream
        // Then: Expecting IllegalArgumentException due to unsupported mark operation
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveInputStream((InputStream) pipedInputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: IllegalArgumentException");
        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveInputStreamWithNullInputStream() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveInputStream with a null InputStream
        // Then: Expecting IllegalArgumentException due to null stream
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveInputStream((InputStream) null);
            fail("Expecting exception: IllegalArgumentException");
        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveInputStreamWithEmptyByteArrayInputStream() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and an empty ByteArrayInputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        ByteArrayInputStream byteArrayInputStream = new ByteArrayInputStream(new byte[0]);

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveInputStream with the ByteArrayInputStream
        // Then: Expecting NoClassDefFoundError due to missing IOUtils class
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveInputStream((InputStream) byteArrayInputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: NoClassDefFoundError");
        } catch (NoClassDefFoundError e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithUnsupportedArchiver() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a MockFileOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        MockFileOutputStream mockFileOutputStream = new MockFileOutputStream("dump");

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveOutputStream with an unsupported archiver type
        // Then: Expecting Exception due to unsupported archiver
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("arj", mockFileOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: Exception");
        } catch (Exception e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithUnsupportedStreamingArchiver() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a ByteArrayOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        ByteArrayOutputStream byteArrayOutputStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream(224);

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveOutputStream with an unsupported streaming archiver
        // Then: Expecting Exception due to unsupported streaming
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("7z", byteArrayOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: Exception");
        } catch (Exception e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithMissingCharsetsClass() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a MockFileOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        MockFileOutputStream mockFileOutputStream = new MockFileOutputStream("dump");

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveOutputStream with a missing Charsets class
        // Then: Expecting NoClassDefFoundError due to missing Charsets class
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("jar", mockFileOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: NoClassDefFoundError");
        } catch (NoClassDefFoundError e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.zip.ZipEncodingHelper", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithMissingCountingOutputStreamClass() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a MockFileOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory("tar");
        MockFileOutputStream mockFileOutputStream = new MockFileOutputStream("zip");

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveOutputStream with a missing CountingOutputStream class
        // Then: Expecting NoClassDefFoundError due to missing CountingOutputStream class
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("tar", mockFileOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: NoClassDefFoundError");
        } catch (NoClassDefFoundError e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

        // Then: Expecting NoClassDefFoundError due to missing CountingOutputStream class
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("tar", mockFileOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: NoClassDefFoundError");
        } catch (NoClassDefFoundError e) {

        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithMissingCharsetsClass2() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a MockFileOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        MockFileOutputStream mockFileOutputStream = new MockFileOutputStream("zip");

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithArArchiver() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a MockFileOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        MockFileOutputStream mockFileOutputStream = new MockFileOutputStream("arj");

        // When: Creating an ArchiveOutputStream with the "ar" archiver
        // Then: Expecting successful creation and verifying LONGFILE_BSD constant
        ArArchiveOutputStream arArchiveOutputStream = (ArArchiveOutputStream) archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("ar", mockFileOutputStream);
        assertEquals(1, ArArchiveOutputStream.LONGFILE_BSD);
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithNullOutputStream() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();

        // Then: Expecting IllegalArgumentException due to null OutputStream
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("7z", (OutputStream) null);
            fail("Expecting exception: IllegalArgumentException");
        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithNullArchiverName() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a PipedOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        PipedOutputStream pipedOutputStream = new PipedOutputStream();

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveOutputStream with a null archiver name
        // Then: Expecting IllegalArgumentException due to null archiver name
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream((String) null, pipedOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: IllegalArgumentException");
        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithMissingCharsetsClass3() throws Throwable {

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveInputStreamWithInvalidArchiverName() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a BufferedInputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        ByteArrayInputStream byteArrayInputStream = new ByteArrayInputStream(new byte[0]);
        DataInputStream dataInputStream = new DataInputStream(byteArrayInputStream);
        BufferedInputStream bufferedInputStream = new BufferedInputStream(dataInputStream);

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveInputStream with an invalid archiver name
        // Then: Expecting Exception due to invalid archiver name
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveInputStream("|V9c}}a_vhb9", (InputStream) bufferedInputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: Exception");
        } catch (Exception e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.ArchiveStreamFactory", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testSetEntryEncodingWithExistingEncoding() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory with an existing encoding
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory("ar");

        // When: Attempting to set the entry encoding with an existing encoding

        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.setEntryEncoding("ar");

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testCreateArchiveOutputStreamWithMissingCharsetsClass9() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory and a ByteArrayOutputStream
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();
        archiveStreamFactory.setEntryEncoding("could not use reset and mark operations.");
        ByteArrayOutputStream byteArrayOutputStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream(224);

        // When: Attempting to create an ArchiveOutputStream with a missing Charsets class
        // Then: Expecting NoClassDefFoundError due to missing Charsets class
        try {
            archiveStreamFactory.createArchiveOutputStream("cpio", byteArrayOutputStream);
            fail("Expecting exception: NoClassDefFoundError");
        } catch (NoClassDefFoundError e) {
            verifyException("org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.zip.ZipEncodingHelper", e);
        }
    }

    @Test(timeout = 4000)
    public void testGetEntryEncoding() throws Throwable {
        // Given: An instance of ArchiveStreamFactory
        ArchiveStreamFactory archiveStreamFactory = new ArchiveStreamFactory();

        // When: Getting the entry encoding
        // Then: Expecting null entry encoding
        String entryEncoding = archiveStreamFactory.getEntryEncoding();
        assertNull(entryEncoding);
    }
